sourceMovie.title: The Hitman's Bodyguard
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit The Hitman's Bodyguard for the same reason it broke through in 2017: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a comedy rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. Patrick Hughes keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The action and comedy blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Bad Boys
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If The Hitman's Bodyguard clicked for you, Bad Boys (1995) is a strong next stop: both trade in action and comedy, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after The Hitman's Bodyguard?
faq.answer: Try Bad Boys, Hitman's Wife's Bodyguard, Smokin' Aces—each shares DNA with The Hitman's Bodyguard while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is The Hitman's Bodyguard?
faq.answer: The Hitman's Bodyguard (2017) is best described as Action, Comedy, Crime, Thriller. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is The Hitman's Bodyguard part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "The Hitman's Bodyguard Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
